message: contract revise can persist a plan that becomes invalid after a non-plan update because plan validation only runs when the plan field itself is revised.
evidence: A paths_in_scope-only revise skips validateContractPlan, so an existing plan.expected_files entry can become out of scope and still be written.

message: Plain contract show drops context path and lines when a context selector also has symbol.
evidence: The renderer's symbol branch prints only the symbol and why text, so path/lines are omitted from the rendered contract markdown.

message: contract revise can accept a paths_in_scope change that makes an existing plan.expected_files entry invalid, because plan validation only runs when the plan field itself is updated.
evidence: contractReviseWithUpdate validates only under update.Plan != nil; applyContractPartialUpdate can still change PathsInScope, and validateContractPlan checks expected_files against contract.PathsInScope.

message: plain contract show omits context path/lines when a context selector also has symbol, so valid plan context fields are not preserved without data loss in rendered output.
evidence: The renderer takes the ctx.Symbol branch and skips the branch that prints ctx.Path and ctx.Lines.

message: TestPlanDAGHashStability does not compare against a frozen pre-slice golden hash as required by the contract.
evidence: The test computes h1 with contractVersionHash in the same test and only compares h2/h3 to h1; no literal frozen golden hash is asserted.

message: TestPlanDAGHashStability derives the expected hash from the current implementation instead of comparing against a frozen pre-slice SHA-256, so plan-less contract hash drift would still pass.
evidence: h1 is assigned from contractVersionHash(contract), then later variants are compared to h1; no frozen golden hash is asserted.

message: TestPlanDAGValid does not assert all plan fields survive show/show --json, and the plain-text depends_on assertion is ambiguous.
evidence: JSON assertions omit expected_files, acceptance, validation, and context Symbol/Why; plain text checks "t1" for depends_on even though t1 is also a task id.

message: The scope-violation revise test does not cover changing paths_in_scope after a valid plan already exists.
evidence: The test sets paths_in_scope first and then revises the plan, while contractReviseWithUpdate only validates plans when update.Plan is non-nil.

message: User-facing contract docs omit the new contract.plan schema and structural validation rules exposed through contract revise and contract show.
evidence: The implementation accepts and validates contract.plan in internal/app/contract.go and renders it in internal/app/run.go, but docs/flow.md's contract revise section documents the editable contract flow without the new plan/tasks schema or rejection cases.
